Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Automatic Stay Pending Appeal Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1110b
Off Calendar Jun-01-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. William P. Donovan Jr. of Cooley LLP, Tel. No. 1-310-883-6435 appearing
for the Petitioner;
Atty. Michael T. Risher of ACLU, Tel. No. 1-415-621-2493 appearing for the Petitioner;
Atty. Enrique A. Monagas Office of the Attorney General, Tel. No. 1-213-897-2483 appearing for the Respndent;
Atty. Christopher W. Magana, Deputy County Counsel, Tel. No. 1-707-565-2421
appearing for the Respondent.


RULING - A R G U E D; PETITIONERS PETER LA FOLLETTE AND THE AMERICAN CIVIL LIBERITIES UNION OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Lift Automatic Stay Pending Appeal Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1110b. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/ru

* * * After the hearing the court issued an Order Denying Plaintiffs' Motion to Lift Automatic Stay Pending Appeal (CCP Section 1110b) * * *

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Apr-03-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PETITIONERS PETER LA FOLLETTE and THE AMERICAN CIVIL LIBERITIES UNION OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR ADMISSION PRO HAC VICE OF JULIE A. EBERSTEIN AS COUNSEL is GRANTED. No opposition filed.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.

Motion For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Mar-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
William Donovan Jr. Esq. 310-883-6435 of Cooley or Petititoner PETER LA FOLLETTE, THE AMERICAN CIVIL LIBERITIES UNION.
Michael Risher Esq. 415-621-2493 of ACLU for Petitioner PETER LA FOLLETTE, THE AMERICAN CIVIL LIBERITIES UNION.
Christopher Magana, Deputy County Counsel 707-565-2421 Office of the County Counsel, county of Sonoma for Respondent William Rousseau.
Enrique Monagas, Deputy Attorney General 213-897-2483 Dept. of Justice, Office of the Atty. General for Respondent Alex Padilla.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court raises several questions to the parties. Both sides aaddress the Court. Plaintiff argues CA Supreme Court case. Defendant submitted objections to the evidence. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER PETER LA FOLLETTE, THE AMERICAN CIVIL LIBERITIES UNION's Motion For Writ Of Mandate is taken under submission.*

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/RBU)

*After the hearing, The Court signs an Order Granting Plaintiff's motion for Writ of Mandate.(see scanned order for details)*
